question.text: When all open positions in a futures contract are required to result in physical delivery on maturity, the settlement type is called:

explanation.text: **The Logic:** Compulsory delivery means ALL outstanding positions at maturity must result in physical delivery — both buyers (take delivery) and sellers (give delivery) are obligated. **The Trap:** 'Both options' requires both parties to agree. Cash settlement has no physical delivery. 'Optional delivery' is not a standard term. **Cross-Exam Context:** Settlement types are specific to commodity derivatives. **Distributor's Pro-Tip:** Gold and Silver on MCX are compulsory delivery — if you hold a position through expiry, you MUST take/give delivery. Plan your exit strategy well in advance.
